pattern: C5 (dataflow merge state hole) — PendingCondInits intersection merge at BSCIRInitAnalysis.cpp:766 drops a delegation PCI created on only one arm of a conditional-expression return; distinct from C15 (call-arg Move-drop, needs _Owned) and the 4 documented §3.7.5 limitations
title: ensure_init_if_ret delegation through a conditional-expression return is wrongly rejected (PendingCondInits intersection merge drops the PCI at the ternary branch join)

pattern: C3 (Visit/switch coverage gap) — checkEnsureInitDerefReads invoked only from the Assign-statement loop, never for terminator operands
title: ensure_init/ensure_init_if_ret pointee read in a terminator operand (call arg / switch-if-while-for discriminant) silently accepted

detail: {"detail": "The PR's VisitCallExpr reset only fires when getAddressedVarDecl(ArgE) returns a VarDecl. For `&s.p` the UnaryOperator's subexpr is a MemberExpr, and getVarDeclFromExpr (BSCNullabilityCheck.cpp:169-183) has NO MemberExpr arm \u2014 it handles only DeclRefExpr/ImplicitCastExpr/ParenExpr/SafeExpr/BinaryOperator \u2014 so getAddressedVarDecl returns nullptr and the escape is silently ignored. Field nullability narrowing lives in CurrStatusFP (not CurrStatusVD), which the reset path (lines 698-701) never touches even if the field were identified. Result: `struct S { int *_Nullable p; }; void mutate(int *_Nullable *_Nonnull pp){ *pp = (int *_Nullable)0; } int f(struct S s){ if (s.p) { mutate(&s.p); return *s.p; } return 0; }` compiles with ZERO diagnostics on the PR compiler (verified), yet `*s.p` is a null dereference at runtime because mutate nulled s.p through &s.p. This is exactly the hazard the PR claims to close (and the manual text it adds \u2014 rule 3 at line 4430, '\u628a\u5b83\u7684\u5730\u5740\u4f20\u7ed9\u4e00\u4e2a\u53ef\u4ee5\u901a\u8fc7\u8be5\u5730\u5740\u5199\u56de\u53ef\u7a7a\u6307\u9488\u7684\u51fd\u6570\u53c2\u6570' \u2014 asserts it is closed, with no field exclusion). The local-variable form is correctly caught (verified: the same code with a local `int *_Nullable q = s.p; if(q){ mutate(&q); return *q; }` emits 'nullable pointer cannot be dereferenced'), isolating the miss to the MemberExpr case. Rule violated: \u00a73.4.2/\u00a73.4.4 flow-narrowing must drop to Nullable when the pointer's address escapes to a writable possibly-null pointee. Severity medium: a memory-safety hole (accepted null deref) on realistic input (by-value struct with a nullable pointer field), but a coverage gap in a partial fix rather than a regression of previously-correct behavior.", "path": "clang/lib/Analysis/BSC/BSCNullabilityCheck.cpp", "severity": "medium", "title": "Address-escape narrowing drop is bypassed for struct fields (&s.p), leaving a null deref accepted"}
title: Address-escape narrowing drop is bypassed for struct fields (&s.p), leaving a null deref accepted
